sequences.
Throw few character deaths for good measure and you’ve got the basis significant chunk what you’ve paid your money for.
really did feel cheated what the studio had wanted see advance.
this just modern trend?
checked out trailers for <hide>’s Raiders the Lost Ark, and <hide>’s Goldfinger get bigger picture.
Both revealed some well-known scenes (including Goldfinger’s iconic laser and dialogue), but the plot basics were instead explained voiceover, rather than any especially huge visual giveaways.
Besides, the chance actually seeing these trailers was significantly lessened due the technology available the time release.
Which got thinking further.
‘If you don’t like it, don’t watch it’, hear you cry.
wish were that simple.
the age the internet, exposure has increased tenfold.
Marketing campaigns target social media and television, while the days simple poster are gone.
Why commission still image when you can display scenes from the film screen station other public place?
Not only that, but trailers are also forced upon you the cinema itself before other films.
Without blindfold and soundproof booth hide you have little choice.
It’s not that have problem with marketing and ads.
But they’re spoiling th

experience.
don’t want see all the best bits wrapped into two minutes, six months ahead release.
Have you had film spoiled its trailer/marketing?
Hollywood revealing too much desperate attempt put bums seats?
ticket prices ruin your film-going experience?
Something George mentioned earlier about Terminator trailer jogged memory about another film from the same franchise, Terminator Genisys.
promote the film, trailer had been released that included major plot twist ACTUALLY the trailer.
Now, had heard about this trailer and had been purposely avoiding it.
However, even when you try avoid spoilers, sometimes it’s out your control.
When went see Mad Max, they showed the afore-mentioned trailer and before realised, was too late.
cannot unseen.
Even the director, Alan Taylor, was unhappy about the spoilers the trailer.
Genisys was [spoiler alert] ….. indeed half the inspiration for this convo.
saw that article about Alan Taylor’s thoughts the other day, but could only sympathise much the film was, opinion, travesty.
Have you seen the film now Ryan?
what did you think the big ‘reveal’?
For film that had very little else going for that really did completely spoil the experience for me.
